text: hi Saskia for BBC learning english here
and today i'm going to tell you about
how we use verbs of perception in
english there are a number of these
verbs but i'm just going to look at two
with you now see and here when we use
these verbs this is the form first the
verb of perception then the object of
the sentence then we have either the
infinitive without to or the ing form
here's an example I heard him go down
the stairs or I heard him going down the
stairs there's a slight difference
between these two forms when we use the
infinitive it suggests that we hear or
see the whole action or event using the
ing form suggest that we hear or see
something in progress I saw her across
the street means I saw her cross from
one side to the other
I saw her crossing the street means I
saw her in the middle on the way across
